mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 200,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,400 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST < 1,400 for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production index below 103.0 combined with rising unemployment signals supply-side weakness and labor market deterioration that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflationary or recessionary environment with simultaneous supply and labor weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where both INDPRO < 103.0 and UNRATE > 4.5%, or joint interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail and food service sales above 485,000 million dollars signals strong consumer demand and discretionary spending that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: consumer-led expansion with sustained purchasing power
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS > 485,000, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index above 0.3% combined with elevated federal funds rate above 2.0% signals persistent inflation and monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where both FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPI monthly change > 0.3%, or joint interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and forward-looking weakness in consumer income and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-stage recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured at quarterly frequency combined with sustained retail sales above 480,000 million dollars signals robust aggregate demand and economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 > 19,200 billion dollars AND RSAFS > 480,000 million dollars
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with strong demand fundamentals
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where both GDPC1 > 19,200 and RSAFS > 480,000, or joint interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
